raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command that finds .java files, computes md5, groups duplicates. Use find, xargs, md5sum, sort, uniq -d? Actually need list of duplicate md5 sums. So: find /testbed -type f -name '*.java' -print0 | xargs -0 md5sum | sort | awk '...'. We can use uniq -d to get duplicates. So: find ... | xargs -0 md5sum | sort | uniq -d -f 1? md5sum output: hash filename. So we want duplicate hashes. Use awk to print hash. Then uniq -d. So: find ... -print0 | xargs -0 md5sum | awk '{print $1}' | sort | uniq -d. That's the list.
